title: -Filling Human Welfare and Community Action Commission Public Sector Vacancies

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution confirming the appointment of Catherine Gelsinger to the Human
Welfare and Community Action Commission (HWCAC).
sections.fiscal_impacts: There are no fiscal impacts associated with this recommendation.
sections.current_situation:
The HWCAC advises City Council on social welfare needs in the community. Failure to
maintain full membership of the HWCAC, which also acts as the Board of the Berkeley
Community Action Agency (CAA), could result in a loss of Community Services Block
Grant (CSBG) funding. Vacancies on the Berkeley CAA Board were noted as a “finding”
during the most recent monitoring audit of this program conducted by the State
Department of Community Services and Development (CSD). The HWCAC has
significant vacancies with only three of nine seats filled after losing several members to
moving out of Berkeley in 2025. The low vacancy rate is a critical issue with CSD and
jeopardizes the City’s funding.
sections.background:
The HWCAC has a nine-seat, tripartite board structure with three low-income elected,
three private sector, and three public sector representatives, which may include officials
or members of business, industry, labor, religious, law enforcement, education, or other
major groups and interests in the community served. The three public sector
representatives are appointed by Berkeley City Council per Berkeley Municipal Code
(BMC) Section 3.78.020 (b); the low-income members are appointed through an
election process per BMC Section 3.78.020 (c); and the private-sector representatives
are identified by existing HWCAC members for appointment by Council per BMC
Section 3.78.020 (d).
City staff and existing HWCAC members coordinate community outreach to encourage
and solicit applications for Council consideration and approval.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/

Filling Human Welfare and Community Action Commission CONSENT CALENDAR
Public Sector Vacancies March 10, 2026
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no known environmental impacts associated with the recommendation of this
report.
sections.rationale:
Failure to maintain full membership of the HWCAC is in violation of the City’s CSBG
revenue contract and may jeopardize future funding (about $300,000 per year).
sections.alternatives:
None.
CONTACT PERSON
Kat Larrowe, Community Services Specialist III, HHCS, (510) 981-7555

Page 2
Page 3 of 5
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
FILLING HUMAN WELFARE AND COMMUNITY ACTION COMMISSION PUBLIC
SECTOR VACANCIES
WHEREAS, the Human Welfare and Community Action Commission (HWCAC) shall
consist of nine members. Three public sector representatives are appointed by Berkeley
City Council per Berkeley Municipal Code (BMC) Section 3.78.020 (b); three low-income
members appointed through an election process per BMC Section 3.78.020 (c); and three
representatives for the private sector identified by existing HWCAC members for
appointment by Council per BMC Section 3.78.020 (d); and
WHEREAS, Berkeley Municipal Code Section 3.78.020 (b) stipulates that three public
sector representatives are appointed to the HWCAC by Berkeley City Council as a whole;
and
WHEREAS, Catherine Gelsinger applied to serve on December 8, 2025.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that
Catherine Gelsinger is appointed to the Human Welfare and Community Action
Commission as public sector representative her term beginning March 11, 2026, ending
March 10, 2030.
